evidence: We have made and may continue to make considerable investments in our omni-channel platform, as well as investments in initiatives designed to leverage evolving technologies, including AI.
Use technology to drive more differentiated experiences and efficiencies by using software, data and AI in practical ways that make it even easier for customers to buy and sell cars.
A customer may interact online via our website and mobile app with Skye (our AI-powered virtual assistant) or with our customer experience consultants via phone, text messages or chat.
We use advanced data science, AI and machine learning capabilities to optimize our business and the customer experience.
We also leverage technology, including data automation, Skye and knowledge management systems, to help us improve efficiency in our Customer Experience Centers (CECs).
Technology related to generative AI is advancing rapidly, and its future impact on the automotive ecosystem, including online shopping behavior, is unknown.

Our failure to realize the benefits associated with our omni-channel platform or initiatives designed to leverage evolving technologies, including AI, could have a material adverse effect on our business, sales and results of operations.
The rapid evolution and increased adoption of AI and other evolving technologies may increase our level of cybersecurity risk... could be enhanced or facilitated by AI.

limitations: No dollar amounts or quantified AI-attributable investment figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 (named but unquantified).

AI narrative concreteness scored 4 based on named deployed use cases (Skye virtual assistant, AI/ML in operations) with operational detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., cost savings, volume handled) were disclosed in the excerpts.
The $200 million SG&A savings goal mentioned in MD&A is not explicitly attributed to AI, so it was not included in capex_mentions.

Risk disclosure scored 4 because multiple company-specific risk scenarios with mechanisms are described (brand/reputation harm from AI use, cybersecurity risk amplified by AI, failure to capture AI investment benefits impacting operations), though no quantification or named governance bodies appear in the excerpts.
